sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NXP’s New UCODE X Delivers Industry-Leading RAIN RFID Performance for High-Volume Applications
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors has launched UCODE X, a new RAIN RFID chip offering industry-leading read and write sensitivity and low power consumption. This innovation supports smaller RAIN RFID labels, enabling high-volume applications in retail, logistics, and healthcare, and helps businesses meet evolving global traceability regulations like the EU Digital Product Passport. The UCODE X's flexible configurations and robust performance aim to accelerate RAIN RFID adoption and improve operational efficiency.

feed.title: AbbVie to Acquire Arizona Manufacturing Facility, Further Strengthening Manufacturing Capabilities in the United States
feed.summary: AbbVie (NYSE: ABBV) has announced an agreement to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, along with associated intellectual property, from West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE: WST). This acquisition is part of AbbVie's commitment to invest over $10 billion in the U.S. and will expand its drug delivery device manufacturing capabilities to support current and next-generation immunology and neuroscience medicines. The transaction is expected to close in mid-2026, with AbbVie planning to hire approximately 200 employees and invest over $175 million in the facility.

feed.title: Summit Creek Advisors LLC Sells 16,450 Shares of Bio-Techne Corp $TECH
feed.summary: Summit Creek Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Bio-Techne Corp by 5.1%, selling 16,450 shares, now holding 303,725 shares valued at approximately $16.9 million. Despite this sale, Bio-Techne remains a significant part of Summit Creek's portfolio, and other institutional investors have recently increased their positions, with 98.95% of the stock owned by hedge funds and institutions. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $70.57, and the company offers a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: First Solar Inc.: The Thin-Film Powerhouse Redefining Utility-Scale Solar
feed.summary: First Solar Inc. is positioning itself as a premium brand in utility-scale solar by focusing on advanced thin-film modules, U.S.-centric manufacturing, and grid-ready projects. The company's unique cadmium telluride (CdTe) technology is optimized for durability, high energy yield in extreme climates, and bankability, distinguishing it from commodity panel makers. Its vertically integrated model, policy alignment with domestic manufacturing incentives, and strong ESG narrative provide a significant competitive advantage over traditional crystalline silicon rivals.

feed.title: Ball Corporation $BALL Shares Purchased by Iridian Asset Management LLC CT
feed.summary: Iridian Asset Management LLC CT significantly increased its stake in Ball Corporation (NYSE:BALL) during the third quarter, raising its holdings by 73.7% to 46,624 shares valued at $2.35 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions, and now institutional investors collectively own 86.51% of Ball's stock. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $62.83, following recent upgrades from Citigroup and Wells Fargo.

feed.title: AbbVie to acquire West Pharmaceutical’s Arizona facility for $175 million By Investing.com
feed.summary: AbbVie announced its definitive agreement to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, from West Pharmaceutical Services for over $175 million. This acquisition, which includes intellectual property and 3.5 mL on-body injector technology, aims to strengthen AbbVie's manufacturing capabilities for immunology and neuroscience medicines, with plans to hire approximately 200 employees. The move aligns with AbbVie’s commitment to invest over $10 billion in the US over the next decade and is expected to close in mid-2026.

feed.title: IHG Hotels and Resorts kicks off 2026 with new summer sale
feed.summary: IHG Hotels & Resorts has launched its "Your Year in Travel" summer sale, offering 25% off stays for IHG One Rewards members and 20% off for non-members. This limited-time promotion covers over 270 participating hotels across various brands and regions, including destinations in Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and Southeast Asia. The sale runs from January 15 to February 4, 2026, for stays between January 18 and August 31, 2026, with additional perks like 500 bonus points for first-time IHG One Rewards app downloaders.

feed.title: New York State Teachers Retirement System Has $55.33 Million Position in Simon Property Group, Inc. $SPG
feed.summary: The New York State Teachers Retirement System increased its stake in Simon Property Group (SPG) by 8.2% in Q3, now holding 294,798 shares valued at $55.33 million. Simon Property Group exceeded quarterly expectations with an EPS of $3.22 and revenue of $1.60 billion, raised its quarterly dividend to $2.20, and holds a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average price target of $191.54. Insider purchases have also been noted, with insiders owning about 8.66% of the company.

feed.title: New York State Teachers Retirement System Has $54.06 Million Stock Holdings in Motorola Solutions, Inc. $MSI
feed.summary: The New York State Teachers Retirement System reduced its stake in Motorola Solutions (MSI) by 11.1% in Q3, now holding 118,228 shares valued at $54.06 million. Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 84.17%, with other major firms like Vanguard and Norges Bank increasing or initiating positions. Analysts generally rate MSI as a "Moderate Buy," and the company recently beat EPS estimates and increased its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: New York State Teachers Retirement System Cuts Holdings in HCA Healthcare, Inc. $HCA
feed.summary: The New York State Teachers Retirement System reduced its stake in HCA Healthcare by 8.8% in Q3, selling 15,122 shares but still holding 156,660 shares valued at approximately $66.8 million. This comes as HCA Healthcare reported strong Q3 earnings, beating expectations, and provided positive FY2025 guidance. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for HCA, with an average price target of $485.95, while corporate insiders also adjusted their holdings.

feed.title: lululemon expects to hit high end of Q4 guidance after strong holidays
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. announced on Monday that it anticipates its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results to hit the high end of its previously issued guidance, driven by strong holiday sales. The company expects net revenue to be at the upper limit of $3.500 billion to $3.585 billion and diluted earnings per share near the top of its $4.66 to $4.76 guidance. This positive outlook comes despite recent challenges and ongoing proxy fights, and the company will meet with analysts at the ICR Conference from January 12-14, 2026.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts PT on Old Dominion Freight Line to $150 From $142, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has increased its price target for Old Dominion Freight Line (NASDAQ: ODFL) to $150 from $142, while maintaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a modest upward revision in the analyst's valuation of the freight company. The article also lists recent analyst price target changes for Old Dominion Freight Line from other firms.

feed.title: Senate report says UnitedHealth used aggressive tactics to boost medicare payments, WSJ reports By Reuters
feed.summary: A U.S. Senate committee report, cited by the Wall Street Journal, indicates that UnitedHealth Group employed aggressive tactics to increase Medicare Advantage payments by collecting specific diagnoses. Shares of UnitedHealth dipped in premarket trading following the report. However, the report does not make formal recommendations or accuse the company of wrongdoing.

feed.title: Impinj licenses Gen2X technology to EM Microelectronic
feed.summary: Impinj has announced a licensing agreement with EM Microelectronic to integrate its Gen2X technology into future EM endpoint integrated circuits. This collaboration is expected to lead to the commercial availability of next-generation, Gen2X-enabled dual-frequency ICs by 2027. The agreement aims to advance ultra-low power and low voltage integrated circuit design.

feed.title: Strong holiday season puts lululemon’s quarter near top of its targets
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. (NASDAQ: LULU) announced updated expectations for its fiscal 2025 fourth quarter, projecting net revenue and diluted earnings per share to be at the higher end of its previously guided ranges. This positive revision is attributed to strong performance during the holiday period. The company will further discuss its outlook with analysts and investors at the ICR Conference from January 12-14, 2026.

feed.title: The Truth About NVR Inc.: The Quiet Stock Wall Street Won’t Shut Up About
feed.summary: This article examines NVR Inc. (NVR), a homebuilder that operates with a disciplined, asset-light, and pre-sold focus, distinguishing it from competitors. While it lacks the viral hype of meme stocks, it garners respect from serious investors for its consistent performance and resilient business model, especially in a fluctuating housing market. The article assesses NVR's business model, risk level, and long-term potential, concluding it's a "cop" for long-term wealth builders who understand housing cycle risks, but a "drop" for short-term traders.

feed.title: Senate report says UnitedHealth used aggressive tactics to boost medicare payments, WSJ reports
feed.summary: A U.S. Senate committee investigating UnitedHealth Group's practices has reported that the health insurer used aggressive tactics to gather diagnoses aimed at boosting Medicare Advantage payments. The report, cited by the Wall Street Journal, does not accuse UnitedHealth of wrongdoing nor make formal recommendations. Following the news, UnitedHealth's shares saw a 1.4% decline in premarket trading.

feed.title: Cummins Inc. Reinvents the Powertrain: How a 105?Year?Old Engine Giant Is Turning Into a Clean-Tech
feed.summary: Cummins Inc. is transforming from a diesel engine manufacturer into a diversified clean-power platform, focusing on hydrogen, batteries, and low-carbon engines to adapt to the electrifying transport market. The company is rebuilding its portfolio into a modular power platform under the Accelera by Cummins brand, offering advanced diesel, natural gas, hydrogen internal combustion, fuel cells, and battery-electric systems. This strategic shift aims to provide versatile powertrain solutions for various regulatory futures, positioning Cummins as an integrated supplier across multiple energy pathways.

feed.title: Moderna chair warns of ‘dystopia’ amid US science pullback
feed.summary: Noubar Afeyan, co-founder of Moderna, has warned that the politicization of science and the US pullback from scientific funding and research are creating a "dystopia." He highlighted the paradox where medical advancements can cure dire genetic conditions, yet preventable diseases like measles are re-emerging due to "corrosive doubt" and a rejection of scientific principles. Afeyan lamented the decline of the US as a driving force for scientific research and the increasing skepticism towards established scientific methods.

feed.title: Koninklijke Philips N.V.: How a 133?Year?Old Giant Is Rebooting Healthcare Tech
feed.summary: Koninklijke Philips N.V. is transforming from a consumer electronics brand into a focused health-technology platform, emphasizing diagnostic imaging, patient monitoring, and connected care powered by cloud software and AI. The company aims to address healthcare challenges like clinician shortages and data fragmentation by offering integrated solutions and long-term partnerships rather than standalone devices. This strategic pivot positions Philips to compete with industry giants like GE HealthCare, Siemens Healthineers, and Medtronic, with its stock performance closely tied to successful execution of this health-tech roadmap.

feed.title: Data center investments to hit $3 trillion over next five years, says Moody’s By Investing.com
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings projects that approximately $3 trillion will be invested in data-center-related infrastructure over the next five years. This substantial capital influx is driven by the booming demand from artificial intelligence and cloud computing, with major tech companies leading the investment surge. The report also highlights the growing participation of institutional investors beyond banks and the increasing use of varied financing structures like asset-backed securities for data center projects.

feed.title: Fox Corp. (Class B) stock: quiet chart, noisy headlines and a market trying to pick a side
feed.summary: Fox Corp. (Class B) stock is drifting in a tight range, despite fresh political headlines and streaming pivots. The company's stock has a modest capital loss of about 4.1 percent over the past year. Wall Street analysts maintain a cautious "Hold" rating, with some seeing limited upside due to resilient live news and sports content, while others highlight structural pressures on pay-TV subscribers.

feed.title: West to sell SmartDose 3.5mL system to AbbVie for $112.5 million
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) announced an agreement to sell the manufacturing and supply rights for its SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System to AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) for $112.5 million. This transaction, expected to close in mid-2026, includes associated facilities and represents about 4% of West's anticipated 2025 revenue. AbbVie's acquisition aligns with its strategic expansion in drug delivery, while West will continue commercial supply until closing and retain rights for other SmartDose versions.

feed.title: Principal Financial's Q4 2025 Earnings: What to Expect
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group (PFG) is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings, with analysts expecting a 15% increase in adjusted EPS to $2.23. Despite a recent earnings miss, the company's shares have gained 15.3% over the past 52 weeks, though trailing the S&P 500. Analysts currently hold a "Hold" rating on PFG stock.

feed.title: A Look At Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) Valuation After DeepSee Agentic AI Partnership Expansion
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) has expanded its partnership with DeepSee, investing in the company to integrate agentic AI into post-trade operations. While the stock is up only 1.3% year-to-date, Simply Wall St's analysis suggests it is undervalued by 17.1% based on core business strength, yet its P/E ratio is higher than industry averages, indicating the market is already paying a premium for its quality and AI initiative.

feed.title: Home Depot Goes All-In On Agentic AI With Google Cloud
feed.summary: Home Depot announced an expanded strategic partnership with Google Cloud, focusing on agentic AI tools to provide real-time, expert guidance to customers. Leveraging Google Cloud's Gemini models, Home Depot aims to create a personalized "AI-first" experience through enhancements like the Magic Apron assistant and AI-powered product list builders. This initiative extends to Google Search's AI Mode and the Gemini app, ensuring home improvement expertise is accessible wherever customers are.

feed.title: USA Compression Partners stock price target raised to $25 from $23 at Mizuho
feed.summary: Mizuho increased its price target for USA Compression Partners (NYSE: USAC) from $23 to $25, while maintaining a Neutral rating. The firm noted USA Compression's outperformance against peers, attributed to positive natural gas macro trends and increased exposure to dry gas basins. Recent performance includes a 14.5% one-year total return and a "nicely accretive" acquisition of J-W Power, which is expected to accelerate deleveraging and improve margins.

feed.title: Walmart Drone Delivery Stores Set to Double to Cover Over 10% of Americans
feed.summary: Walmart plans to significantly expand its drone delivery service, partnering with Wing to add 150 more stores, bringing the total to over 270 locations. This expansion, covering major metropolitan areas like Los Angeles, Cincinnati, St. Louis, and Miami, aims to serve over 40 million Americans, roughly 10% of the US population, by the end of 2027. The move follows a reported tripling of drone deliveries from existing locations in the last six months of 2025.

feed.title: JPMorgan Adjusts PT on Expeditors International of Washington to $132 From $134, Maintains Underweight Rating
feed.summary: JPMorgan has revised its price target for Expeditors International of Washington (EXPD) to $132 from $134, while reiterating an Underweight rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a bearish outlook from the firm despite other analysts like BofA and Wolfe making various adjustments to their ratings and price targets for the logistics company. The article also provides a company profile, financial data, and analyst consensus information for Expeditors International.

feed.title: Tempus AI climbs on $1.1 billion contract value
feed.summary: Tempus AI has seen a climb in its stock value following the announcement of a $1.1 billion contract. The news, reported by Reuters, is a headline-only item indicating the significant financial development for the company.

feed.title: MGG.DE MGM Resorts (XETRA) +13.99% intraday 12 Jan 2026: earnings catalysts
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International (MGG.DE) saw an intraday surge of 13.99% on XETRA, driven by better-than-expected quarterly results, renewed optimism for travel demand, and sector rotation into entertainment stocks. While short-term momentum looks strong with the stock trading above key moving averages, Meyka AI's forecast model projects a significant downside in the long term. Investors are advised to consider both the immediate trading opportunities and longer-term risks like debt levels and margin pressure.

feed.title: Targa Resources stock price target raised to $196 by Goldman Sachs
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has increased its price target for Targa Resources (NYSE:TRGP) to $196 while maintaining a Buy rating, citing strong Permian volume growth and successful acquisitions. The investment bank's EBITDA forecasts for Targa in Q4 2025, full-year 2025, and 2026 are largely in line with or above company guidance and consensus, driven by strategic acquisitions and anticipated growth in the Permian basin. Other analysts from UBS, Wells Fargo, and RBC Capital have also raised their price targets for Targa Resources, reflecting positive sentiment around the company's recent strategic moves and operational performance.

feed.title: Why is American Express's Shares Dipping in Premarket? Trump Threatens Interest Rate Caps
feed.summary: American Express shares fell in premarket trading after former President Donald Trump proposed a 10% cap on credit card interest rates. This announcement, though not yet law, caused a broad selloff across the financial services sector, impacting major banks and credit card companies significantly. Analysts warn that such a cap could severely hurt revenue and profit for issuers, potentially leading to increased fees and reduced credit availability.

feed.title: Bank of America discloses 1.43% stake in Avadel Pharmaceuticals
feed.summary: Bank of America has disclosed a 1.43% stake in Avadel Pharmaceuticals plc, as revealed in a recent regulatory filing under Irish Takeover Panel Act rules. The holding includes both owned shares and cash-settled derivatives, with the bank also maintaining short positions in the company. These transactions, including purchases and sales of shares, were conducted on January 9, 2026.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Announces Executive Leadership Changes
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. has announced significant executive leadership changes, effective early 2026. Michel Lagarde and Frederick M. Lowery are departing, while Marc N. Casper will become Chairman and CEO. Gianluca Pettiti and Michael Shafer will assume new leadership roles, including President & COO for Pettiti.

feed.title: Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions Inc Announces Operating Expense Reductions
feed.summary: Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions Inc (AITX) has announced significant operating expense reductions to improve cash flow. These measures include cutting monthly payroll by $130,000 and reducing other monthly expenses by $50,000, along with pausing R&D on non-core projects. The company aims to achieve positive cash flow by May 2026 through these efforts.

feed.title: QXO Signs Investment Agreement Joinders With Convertible Preferred Investors
feed.summary: QXO has entered into joinders to its Investment Agreement with Convertible Preferred Investors, including a fund managed by affiliates of Apollo, to expand its Series C Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock financing. New investors have committed to purchase up to 185,500 shares at $10,000 per share, totaling $1.855 billion, with commitments valid through July 15, 2026. This upsizing brings the aggregate commitments to $3.0 billion and aims to strengthen QXO’s capital base and financial flexibility.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts PT on Teledyne Technologies to $579 From $584, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has adjusted its price target for Teledyne Technologies (TDY) to $579, down from $584, while maintaining an Equalweight rating on the stock. This update reflects a minor revision in the analyst's valuation of the company. The article also provides a summary of recent news and financial data related to Teledyne Technologies.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Price Target on Paycom Software to $185 From $210, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target on Paycom Software Inc. (PAYC) to $185 from $210, while maintaining an "Equalweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment comes after other financial institutions like Citigroup, UBS, KeyBanc, Baird, BMO Capital, Guggenheim, TD Cowen, Deutsche Bank, Stifel, Jefferies, JPMorgan, and Mizuho also revised their price targets for Paycom in recent months. The company, which specializes in cloud-based human resources management software, recently reported its Q3 2025 earnings with rising non-GAAP earnings and revenue but slower quarterly revenue growth.

feed.title: Earnings Scheduled For January 12, 2026
feed.summary: This article lists companies scheduled to report earnings on January 12, 2026. Sify Technologies (NASDAQ: SIFY) is expected to report a quarterly loss of $0.01 per share on revenue of $171.10 million. Wealthfront (NASDAQ: WLTH) is estimated to report quarterly earnings of $0.20 per share on revenue of $93.18 million.

feed.title: Dollar Tree Signal 12/01: Plunging Margins (Chart)
feed.summary: This article analyzes Dollar Tree (NASDAQ: DLTR) stock, suggesting a short trade opportunity due to plunging margins and high valuations despite a strong 2025 rally. The analysis highlights bearish technical indicators, elevated valuations, and concerns regarding sustained growth and profitability outlooks. The author provides specific entry, take profit, and stop loss levels for a short position.

feed.title: AbbVie to buy Arizona manufacturing facility from West Pharmaceutical (WST:NYSE)
feed.summary: AbbVie (ABBV) has announced an agreement to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, along with related intellectual property, from West Pharmaceutical Services (WST). West Pharmaceutical will divest all manufacturing and supply rights for its SmartDose 3.5mL On-Body Delivery System as part of this transaction. This acquisition marks a strategic move for AbbVie to enhance its manufacturing capabilities.

feed.title: AbbVie to acquire West Pharmaceutical’s Arizona facility for $175 million
feed.summary: AbbVie announced its intent to acquire a device manufacturing facility in Tempe, Arizona, from West Pharmaceutical Services for $175 million. This acquisition, which includes associated intellectual property and 3.5 mL on-body injector technology, aims to strengthen AbbVie's manufacturing capabilities for immunology and neuroscience medicines and expand its U.S. presence. The transaction is expected to close in mid-2026 and aligns with AbbVie’s commitment to invest over $10 billion in the U.S.

feed.title: KLA Corp. price target raised to $1,500 from $1,125 at Susquehanna
feed.summary: Susquehanna has increased its price target for KLA Corp. (KLAC) to $1,500 from $1,125, maintaining a Neutral rating. This adjustment comes as channel checks indicate the semiconductor capital equipment industry is poised for significant spending in wafer fab equipment, expecting global semi revenues to exceed $1 trillion. The firm believes that fundamental trends in wafer fab equipment remain strong, necessitating substantial investment in the supply chain.

feed.title: ‘Could have been a hell of a lot worse’: At Nebraska plant, ADM allowed dangerous dust to build up beyond safe levels, recordings show
feed.summary: A 2022 dust explosion at an ADM plant in Fremont, Nebraska, revealed that the facility had accumulated combustible grain dust eight times the acceptable federal limit. Newly obtained OSHA interview recordings show that senior ADM managers were unaware of federal safety standards, few employees were disciplined for safety lapses, and dangerous cleaning practices like using compressed air on running machinery were common. ADM paid a fine of about $41,000 for the incident, and this event is part of a pattern of dust explosions at ADM facilities across the Midwest, with at least eight documented between 2018 and 2024.

feed.title: 3 Funds for 2026 and Beyond
feed.summary: This article recommends three funds—Vanguard Dividend Growth (VDIGX), Artisan International Value (ARTKX), and Parnassus Core Equity (PRBLX)—as sensible long-term investments for 2026 and beyond. Author Russel Kinnel suggests these funds, despite subpar 2025 returns, offer defensive characteristics and quality holdings, making them suitable for navigating market uncertainties and potential economic shifts. He emphasizes a long-term investment horizon of a decade or more for these selections.

feed.title: A Fidelity Global Fund That Brings Together Distinct Styles and Top Talent
feed.summary: Fidelity Global Equity+ F is a two-year-old fund of funds combining four existing Fidelity strategies, driven by managers Mark Schmehl, Hugo Lavallée, and Daniel Dupont. The fund, which has an ETF version (FGEP), is rated High for People and Above Average for Process due to its blend of styles and experienced managers. Despite its short history, the strategy has delivered strong annualized returns, outpacing its global equity peers, but its significant Canadian allocation presents both opportunity and risk.

feed.title: Intuit Signs Credit Agreement With JPMorgan Chase Bank and Other Lenders
feed.summary: Intuit has signed a new Credit Agreement for an unsecured $2.2 billion revolving credit facility with JPMorgan Chase Bank and other lenders. This five-year facility, maturing on January 9, 2031, can be increased by up to $4.0 billion subject to approval and is intended to support working capital, early refund processing, and other general corporate purposes. The borrowings will accrue interest based on base rates or SOFR, plus a margin tied to credit ratings.

feed.title: Pelican Acquisition Corp Announces Business Combination Details
feed.summary: Pelican Acquisition Corp (PELI) announced details of its business combination with Greenland Exploration Limited and other entities on January 9, 2026. Key actions include a video documentary for investor presentations, the filing of a registration statement, and upcoming proxy statements for shareholder voting. The company highlighted potential risks and benefits associated with forward-looking statements.

feed.title: Jaguar Health Signs Securities Purchase Agreements With Accredited Investors
feed.summary: Jaguar Health (JAGX) has entered into Securities Purchase Agreements with two accredited investors to issue $350,000 of unsecured promissory notes and accompanying stock warrants. The notes carry a 6% interest rate and mature one month after issuance, while the warrants cover up to 350,000 shares at a $1.00 exercise price. The proceeds from this transaction, which closed on January 5, 2026, will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
